mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month momentum in retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term earnings support, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS growth is stronger.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day return spread versus lower RSAFS growth, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates cross materially above recent norms while inflation remains firm, equity discount-rate pressure dominates nominal revenue support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average by at least 50bp while CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation exceeds its 12-month rate
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and weaker expected consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days after labor-market weakening becomes visible.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses 0.3pp above its trailing 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after the UNRATE trigger are not lower than non-triggered returns, or if the downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A housing downturn combined with restrictive short rates transmits through construction, credit quality, and household wealth channels, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% year over year while FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average
regime_hint: credit-sensitive housing contraction under restrictive policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS trigger does not underperform the unconditional 60-day return by at least 100bp on average in walk-forward out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production signals weakening real activity and operating leverage pressure for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after production momentum turns down.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO 3-month growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or has Rank IC above -0.03 in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate paired with continued payroll growth identifies benign easing rather than recessionary easing, reducing discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls at least 50bp over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month change remains positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint easing-with-payroll-growth condition fails to generate positive average 20-day returns or does not outperform easing periods with negative PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real GDP growth indicates broad demand improvement that lifts expected earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as GDPC1 momentum strengthens.
failure_condition: Reject if higher two-quarter GDPC1 growth acceleration does not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than GDP deceleration, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A short-run acceleration in consumer prices raises expected policy restraint and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after inflation momentum jumps.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses above its trailing 12-month inflation rate by at least 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL accelerations are followed by non-negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if downside hit rate is below 52% in out-of-sample tests.
